mission.title: Install Kuadrant on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kuadrant is a Kubernetes operator that enhances Gateway API providers (Istio or Envoy Gateway) with policy capabilities including TLS management, DNS routing, application authentication (AuthPolicy), and rate limiting (RateLimitPolicy). The Kuadrant operator is installed via Helm and requires Gateway API CRDs, cert-manager, and a gateway provider as prerequisites.

steps.title: Install Gateway API CRDs (prerequisite)
steps.description:
Kuadrant requires Gateway API CRDs to be installed on your cluster:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api/releases/download/v1.2.1/standard-install.yaml
```

steps.title: Install cert-manager (prerequisite)
steps.description:
Kuadrant requires cert-manager for TLS certificate management:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/latest/download/cert-manager.yaml
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=120s deployment/cert-manager -n cert-manager
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=120s deployment/cert-manager-webhook -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Install a Gateway provider (prerequisite)
steps.description:
Kuadrant works with either Istio or Envoy Gateway. Install one of them:

**Option A — Istio:**
```bash
curl -L https://istio.io/downloadIstio | sh -
istioctl install --set profile=minimal -y
```

**Option B — Envoy Gateway:**
```bash
helm install eg oci://docker.io/envoyproxy/gateway-helm --version v1.2.6 -n envoy-gateway-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Install the Kuadrant Operator via Helm
steps.description:
Add the Kuadrant Helm repository and install the operator:
```bash
helm repo add kuadrant https://kuadrant.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update
helm install kuadrant-operator kuadrant/kuadrant-operator --namespace kuadrant-system --create-namespace --version v1.4.1
```
Wait for the operator to be ready:
```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=120s deployment/kuadrant-operator-controller-manager -n kuadrant-system
```

steps.title: Create the Kuadrant custom resource
steps.description:
Create a Kuadrant CR to activate the operator's reconciliation of policy resources:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kuadrant.io/v1beta1
kind: Kuadrant
metadata:
  name: kuadrant
  namespace: kuadrant-system
spec: {}
EOF
```
Verify the Kuadrant CR is ready:
```bash
kubectl get kuadrant kuadrant -n kuadrant-system -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}' | jq .
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all Kuadrant components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kuadrant-system
```
You should see the Kuadrant operator controller manager and Limitador (rate limiting) pods running.

Verify the CRDs were installed:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep kuadrant
```
You should see AuthPolicy, RateLimitPolicy, DNSPolicy, TLSPolicy, and Kuadrant CRDs.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Kuadrant operator and Limitador pods running in the kuadrant-system namespace. The Kuadrant CR should show ready status. You can now create AuthPolicy, RateLimitPolicy, DNSPolicy, and TLSPolicy resources targeting your Gateway API HTTPRoutes and Gateways.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kuadrant https://kuadrant.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update
helm install kuadrant-operator kuadrant/kuadrant-operator --namespace kuadrant-system --create-namespace --version v1.4.1

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kuadrant.io/v1beta1
kind: Kuadrant
metadata:
  name: kuadrant
  namespace: kuadrant-system
spec: {}
EOF

uninstall.title: Delete the Kuadrant CR
uninstall.description:
Remove the Kuadrant custom resource first:
```bash
kubectl delete kuadrant kuadrant -n kuadrant-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Kuadrant operator Helm release:
```bash
helm uninstall kuadrant-operator -n kuadrant-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove Kuadrant CRDs and the namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep kuadrant | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete namespace kuadrant-system
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade via Helm
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repo and upgrade to a new version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kuadrant-operator kuadrant/kuadrant-operator --namespace kuadrant-system --version <new-version>
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the operator pod is running the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kuadrant-system
kubectl describe deployment kuadrant-operator-controller-manager -n kuadrant-system | grep Image
```

troubleshooting.title: Operator pod in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check the operator logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n kuadrant-system deployment/kuadrant-operator-controller-manager
```
Common causes: missing Gateway API CRDs, missing cert-manager, or no gateway provider (Istio/Envoy Gateway) installed.

troubleshooting.title: Kuadrant CR not becoming ready
troubleshooting.description:
Check the Kuadrant CR status:
```bash
kubectl get kuadrant kuadrant -n kuadrant-system -o yaml
```
Ensure all prerequisites are installed and the operator pod is running.

troubleshooting.title: RateLimitPolicy not enforcing
troubleshooting.description:
If rate limiting is not working:
1. Check Limitador pod is running: `kubectl get pods -n kuadrant-system -l app=limitador`
2. Verify the RateLimitPolicy targets a valid HTTPRoute: `kubectl get ratelimitpolicy -A`
3. Check operator logs for reconciliation errors

troubleshooting.title: Gateway API CRDs missing
troubleshooting.description:
If the operator shows errors about missing resources:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep gateway
```
Install Gateway API CRDs: `kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api/releases/download/v1.2.1/standard-install.yaml`

prerequisites.description: Kubernetes 1.24+ cluster with: Gateway API CRDs installed, cert-manager running, and a gateway provider (Istio or Envoy Gateway) deployed. Helm 3 and kubectl must be configured to access your cluster.
